content.claim: A 2007 wave of rural school closures and mergers in Hungary, including cases in Nógrád county (Mátraszőlős, Erdőkürt), showed closure cascades where neighboring villages with enrollments of 35–65 pupils operated mixed-year classes, with nearby surviving schools facing 'association pressure' (társulás) as child numbers fell.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Limited to a single micro-region and time period; generalization to national pattern is inferential
Does not establish a causal link between closure and subsequent demographic change in those specific villages
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
